methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $88M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Lohum Cleantech is a Series C-stage climate and energy company that has raised $87.7M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer-comparable set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M), supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private and 1 public comparable in the climate and energy sector.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $88M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $149M. The Peer-Median method takes the median valuation across the 3-company comparable set at $1.0B, with observed peer valuations ranging from $700M to $3.0B. These two methods produce materially different signals, reflecting the wide dispersion in climate tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $127M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median point of $575M. The substantial gap between stage-based and peer-based estimates—$149M versus $1.0B—reflects core uncertainty around whether Lohum's business model, traction, or capital efficiency warrants positioning near the median of Series C climate tech peers or materially below it. Key drivers of resolution will include revenue growth, unit economics, and path to commercialization relative to the peer set.
